title: After-Action Report — Cricket Batting Order: the earned, use-or-lose manager decision
scope: The first cut of the Cricket Batting Order rule flipped the order automatically at the end of every joker-free trip. The user reframed it: the flip is earned, use-or-lose, and a manager decision, and it is regulation-only. A joker-free trip earns one flip (1-9 → 9-1). It's use-or-lose at the top of the next cycle, and whether to spend it is a manager choice driven by a flip-aggression persona and the game situation (score + out-arc). Deploying a joker forfeits the chance to earn one, so jokers and flips trade off.

rationale.item: "There is no use-or-lose [aggregation]. You get one if you cycle through the lineup without using a joker, and it's use-or-lose with the first batter of the new cycle … there are managers who prefer to use it … teams that use jokers more liberally would be less inclined to use this … and a segment who use both situationally based on the score and where in the arc of outs we are."
Plus a separate instruction: regulation only.

delegation_events.decision: Reframe the flip as earned, use-or-lose, manager decision
delegation_events.quote: The user reframed it: the flip is **earned, use-or-lose, and a manager decision**, and it is **regulation-only**.

actions.description: Engine earn/decide/apply split: cricket_order.py gains cricket_order_on, can_flip, flip_line, apply_flip; state.py Team.advance_lineup arms Team.pending_flip on a joker-free wrap and adds Team.mgr_flip_aggression; game.py run_half clears pending_flip at half start; prob.py _maybe_cricket_flip consumes the pending flip and returns a cricket_flip event; manager.py should_use_flip, joker_flip_damp, _in_regulation; pa.py/render.py apply and render the event; config.py CRICKET_FLIP_* and CRICKET_JOKER_FLIP_DAMP tunables

Persona axis mgr_flip_aggression derived inversely from joker_aggression with a leverage nudge (managers.py:_flip_aggression); stored on teams.mgr_flip_aggression (db.py column + migration), persisted in seeding INSERT (league.py), stamped onto the engine Team (sim.py)

risk_posture: Use-or-lose, enforced in two places. The provider clears pending_flip the moment it reaches a decision (used or not); run_half clears it at every half start. So it never banks and never crosses a half boundary.
The joker trade is situational by construction... a genuinely high-leverage spot still clears the lowered bar — that is the "weigh joker-now vs flip-next" behaviour, not a hard veto.

validation_claims.claim: pytest o27/tests/test_cricket_order.py — pending-flip arming, flip helpers, should_use_flip persona/situational monotonicity, joker_flip_damp scaling + inertness, end-to-end
pytest o27/tests — full engine suite green

Provider: regulation spends the flip (event + pending cleared); super-inning declines and still clears it. Live game: flip line names the new leadoff and that hitter bats next; render and no-render paths both show it
validation_claims.check_method: Run a live game with rule on and inspect both log paths

validation_claims.claim: DB fresh + legacy migrations land cricket_order_enabled and mgr_flip_aggression; INSERT counts align 31/31/31
validation_claims.check_method: Run migrations on fresh and legacy DBs and count INSERT columns

non_actions.item: No new stat family; no dismissal/all-out model

open_hooks: Flip-aware lineup construction (delivered in the follow-on AAR)
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Cricket Batting Order: the earned, use-or-lose manager decision
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/o27-cricket-batting-order-k6hhp; precedes docs/aar-cricket-batting-order.md (refined) and docs/aar-cricket-batting-order-flip-aware-lineups.md. Behaviour produced: managers self-sort into flip-minded (hoard jokers, live on the churn), joker-happy (rarely earn or spend a flip), and situational (weigh joker-now vs flip-next). Design decisions worth recording: the flip is a provider-returned event, not a mutation at the wrap (decision needs rng + manager which live in the provider; renderer captures PA context before the provider runs; same shape as joker_insertion / declaration); mgr_flip_aggression derived not authored per-archetype (avoided editing all 17 archetypes and threading a brittle new value through the single, already-partial seeding INSERT). What bit me: the first (automatic) version appended the flip line in pa.py and assumed it would render — it didn't, because run_half discards the raw apply_event log when a Renderer is present and rebuilds the play-by-play from the renderer; the event-based redesign fixes this structurally and the line was verified on both paths.
parser_notes: Date from 'Date completed:' line. 'Precedes:' wording is slightly confusing — this AAR refines the original rule AAR.
